response: Yes, both Daniel Mann and Todd Solondz were directors. Daniel Mann was an American film and television director, while Todd Solondz was an American independent film director known for his dark, thought-provoking, and socially conscious work. Mann directed films such as "A Prairie Home Companion," and Solondz directed films like "Welcome to the Dollhouse," "Storytelling," "Palindromes," and "Life During Wartime."
